title: SenML unpack does not validate bver version number
description:
File: python/src/lichen/senml/codec.py
Lines: 131-154

RFC 8428 Section 4.4 states: 'Implementations receiving Records MUST be able to process Records with a Base Version Identifier less than or equal to the version number the implementation understands.'

This implies records with bver > 10 (the current SenML version) should be rejected since the implementation cannot guarantee correct interpretation.

Currently unpack() accepts any bver value silently:
- bver=999 is accepted without warning
- bver with non-integer type (e.g., string) is also accepted

Impact:
1. Future SenML versions may have different semantics; accepting unknown versions risks misinterpretation
2. A sender could use bver to indicate features the receiver doesn't support
3. Malformed bver values pass silently

Fix: In unpack() or from_cbor_map(), validate bver is an integer in range [1, 10] and raise ValueError otherwise.
close_reason: Added bver range validation in from_cbor_map per RFC 8428 §4.4; verified with custom test vectors, ruff, mypy, and pytest
